title: The Wrong Order
text: Sara and her friend went to a restaurant for lunch. They both ordered soup but the waiter brought them sandwiches. Sara politely told the waiter about the mistake. The waiter apologized and brought them the correct order. Before they left, the manager came over and gave them each a free dessert for the trouble.
key_points: Who went to the restaurant and what did they order?
What did the waiter bring instead?
How did they handle the mistake?
What did the restaurant do at the end to make up for it?

title: The Missing Keys
text: Linda was already late for work when she could not find her car keys. She searched her bag, the kitchen counter, and the sofa. Her son came downstairs and told her he had seen them by the front door. Linda found the keys on a small shelf near the entrance. She thanked her son and rushed out to the car.
key_points: Who was looking for something and why was it urgent?
Where did she search?
Who helped her and what did they say?
Where were the keys found?

title: The Birthday Surprise
text: Tom wanted to surprise his wife for her birthday. He asked her best friend Carol to keep her busy for the afternoon. Tom decorated the house and baked her favorite cake. When his wife came home, all her friends were waiting inside. She was so surprised she started to cry. Tom said it was the best reaction he had ever seen.
key_points: What was Tom trying to do and for whom?
How did he keep his wife away from the house?
What had he done to prepare at home?
How did his wife react when she arrived?

title: The Lost Umbrella
text: Mark was getting ready to leave work when he noticed it was raining heavily outside. He looked for his umbrella but could not find it. His coworker Lisa saw him looking worried and offered to share her umbrella. They walked together to the parking lot. The next morning Mark found his umbrella under his desk.
key_points: What was the problem Mark faced when leaving work?
Who helped him and how?
Where did he eventually find his umbrella?

title: The New Baby
text: The woman next door had a new baby. One afternoon the baby started crying because she was hungry. The mother prepared a bottle of milk for her. But the baby did not want to drink it. The mother checked the bottle and felt that the milk was too warm. She ran the bottle under cold water and tested a few drops on her hand. The baby then drank all of the milk and stopped crying.
key_points: Why was the baby crying?
What did the mother prepare and what was the problem with it?
How did the mother fix the problem?
What happened after she fixed it?

title: The School Play
text: Anna and Bob were acting in their school play. Anna forgot her lines in the middle of the scene. Bob remembered what she was supposed to say and quietly told her. Anna smiled, said her lines, and the play continued. After the show their teacher told them both they had done a wonderful job.
key_points: Who were the two people and what were they doing?
What went wrong during the performance?
How did the other person help?
How did it end and what did the teacher say?

title: The Lobster Dinner
text: Claire and her friend went to a restaurant for dinner and ordered lobster. The waiter brought them the wrong dish by mistake. They told the waiter and he apologized right away. He brought them the lobster they had ordered. At the end of the meal, the waiter brought them a small chocolate cake at no extra charge to make up for the mix-up.
key_points: What did Claire and her friend order at the restaurant?
What went wrong with the order?
How did the waiter respond?
What did the restaurant give them at the end?

title: The Broken Heater
text: One winter morning James woke up and noticed his house was very cold. He checked the heater and found that it had stopped working overnight. He called a repair company and they told him someone could come that afternoon. James borrowed two extra blankets from his neighbor while he waited. The repair person arrived and fixed the heater within an hour.
key_points: What problem did James wake up to?
What did he do to get help?
How did he manage while waiting?
How did the situation get resolved?

title: The Library Book
text: Emily borrowed a book from the library for a school project. She finished the project but forgot to return the book. Three weeks later she received a notice saying the book was overdue. She found the book under her bed and returned it the next morning. The librarian told her there was a small fine but said she was glad the book was back.
key_points: Why did Emily borrow the book and what mistake did she make?
How did she find out about the problem?
Where did she find the book and what did she do next?
What happened when she returned it?

title: The Rainy Picnic
text: David and his family planned a picnic in the park for Saturday afternoon. They packed sandwiches, fruit, and drinks and drove to the park. Just as they spread out the blanket it started to rain. They quickly packed everything back into the car. They drove home and had their picnic on the living room floor instead. The children said it was even more fun that way.
key_points: What had the family planned and what went wrong?
What did they decide to do instead?
How did the children feel about the change of plans?

title: The Stray Cat
text: A woman named Helen noticed a stray cat sitting outside her door one evening. She brought it a small bowl of milk. The cat came back the next evening and the evening after that. After a week Helen decided to bring the cat inside. She took it to the vet, who said it was healthy. Helen kept the cat and named her Luna.
key_points: How did Helen first encounter the cat?
What made her decide to bring it inside?
What did the vet say and what did Helen do in the end?

title: The Flat Tire
text: On her way to a job interview, Rachel noticed her car was driving strangely. She pulled over and saw that she had a flat tire. She called her brother who arrived twenty minutes later with a spare. They changed the tire together as quickly as they could. Rachel arrived at the interview fifteen minutes late but explained what had happened. The interviewer told her not to worry and the interview went very well.
key_points: What happened to Rachel and why was the timing bad?
How did she get help?
What happened when she arrived at her destination?

title: The Forgotten Cake
text: Peter was baking a birthday cake for his daughter. He put it in the oven and went to watch television. He fell asleep on the couch and woke up to the smell of burning. He ran to the kitchen and took the cake out of the oven. The top was burnt but the inside was still fine. He cut off the burnt part, added extra frosting, and his daughter said it was the best cake she had ever had.
key_points: What was Peter making and what mistake did he make?
How did he discover the problem?
How did he save the situation?
How did his daughter react?

title: The Wrong Bus
text: On her first day in a new city, Maria got on the wrong bus by mistake. She rode it for several stops before realizing she was going the wrong way. She got off and asked a woman at the bus stop for help. The woman walked her to the correct stop and waited with her until the right bus came. Maria thanked her and arrived at her destination only a few minutes late.
key_points: What mistake did Maria make and how did she realize it?
Who helped her and what did they do?
How did the situation turn out?

title: The Closed Restaurant
text: Kevin and his girlfriend drove across town to try a new restaurant they had heard about. When they arrived they saw a sign on the door saying the restaurant was closed for a private event. They stood outside feeling disappointed. A woman inside noticed them and came to the door. She explained the situation and gave them a voucher for a free meal on their next visit. Kevin said it almost made the trip worth it.
key_points: What did Kevin and his girlfriend find when they arrived?
How did someone from the restaurant respond?
What did the restaurant give them and how did Kevin feel?

title: The Swimming Lesson
text: Eight-year-old Sam was afraid of the water and refused to take swimming lessons. His parents signed him up anyway. On the first day his instructor let him stand in the shallow end and just get used to the water. By the third lesson Sam was putting his face in. By the end of the month he could swim the full length of the pool. He told his parents he wanted to join the swim team.
key_points: Why was Sam signed up for lessons and how did he feel about it?
How did the instructor approach his fear at the start?
What progress did he make over time?
What did he tell his parents at the end?

title: The Early Train
text: Sophie needed to catch an early train to visit her parents. She set two alarms to make sure she would not oversleep. In the morning both alarms went off and she got up straight away. She arrived at the station with twenty minutes to spare. She bought a coffee and a newspaper and sat down to wait. The train arrived on time and she was at her parents' house before noon.
key_points: What did Sophie do to make sure she would not oversleep?
How early did she arrive and what did she do while waiting?
How did the journey go?

title: The Noisy Neighbor
text: For two weeks a man named Greg could hear loud music coming from the apartment next door every night. He finally knocked on his neighbor's door and politely explained the problem. His neighbor was very apologetic and said she had not realized how loud it was. She turned the music down and the two of them ended up talking for almost an hour. After that they became good friends.
key_points: What problem had Greg been dealing with and for how long?
How did he handle it?
How did the neighbor respond?
What unexpected thing happened as a result?

title: The Glasses
text: An elderly man named Harold could not find his reading glasses anywhere in the house. He looked in every room and checked all his usual spots. His granddaughter came to visit and within two minutes found them sitting on top of the bookshelf. Harold laughed and said he must have put them up there without thinking. His granddaughter made him a little sign that said glasses go here and taped it to his nightstand.
key_points: What was Harold's problem and what did he do about it?
Who solved the problem and how quickly?
Where were the glasses found?
What did his granddaughter do to help him in the future?

title: The Garden Project
text: In the spring a woman named Ruth decided to plant a vegetable garden for the first time. She bought seeds, soil, and small pots and set everything up on her balcony. She watered the plants every morning before work. By summer she had tomatoes, peppers, and fresh herbs growing. She brought some tomatoes to her neighbor who said they were the best she had ever tasted.
key_points: What did Ruth decide to do for the first time and what did she need?
What did she do every day to take care of it?
What did she grow and what did she do with some of it?
How did her neighbor react?

title: The Spelling Bee
text: A boy named Leo entered his school spelling bee for the first time. He studied every night for two weeks. On the day of the competition he spelled the first four words correctly. On the fifth word he made a small mistake and was eliminated. He felt disappointed but his teacher told him he had done better than most first-time competitors. Leo said he would enter again next year.
key_points: What did Leo do to prepare and how long did he prepare?
How did the competition go for him?
How did he feel and what did his teacher say?
What did Leo decide about the future?

title: The Power Outage
text: One evening the power went out in the whole street. A woman named Grace lit some candles and found a board game in the closet. She played the game with her two children for two hours. When the power came back on the children asked if they could keep playing instead of watching television. Grace said yes and they played until bedtime.
key_points: What happened and how did Grace respond?
What did the family do during the outage?
What did the children ask for when the power came back?

title: The Thank You Note
text: A man named Victor received a gift from his aunt for his birthday. He meant to send a thank you note but kept forgetting. Three weeks later his aunt called to check if he had received the package. Victor felt embarrassed and apologized. He wrote the note that same evening and mailed it the next morning. His aunt called again when she received it and said it made her day.
key_points: What had Victor forgotten to do and for how long?
What prompted him to finally act?
What did he do and when?
How did his aunt respond?

title: The Lost Wallet
text: A teenager named Mia lost her wallet at the shopping center. She retraced her steps but could not find it. She went to the information desk to report it. An hour later the desk called her name over the loudspeaker. Someone had found the wallet and turned it in with everything still inside. Mia thanked the staff and went home relieved.
key_points: Where did Mia lose her wallet and what did she do first?
What did she do when she could not find it?
How did the story get resolved?

title: The Early Morning Run
text: A woman named Diana decided to start running every morning before work. On the first day she could only run for five minutes before stopping. She kept going each day even when she did not feel like it. After one month she could run for thirty minutes without stopping. Her coworker noticed she seemed more energetic and asked what had changed. Diana told her about the running and her coworker decided to join her.
key_points: What did Diana decide to do and how did it start?
How did she stay consistent?
What progress did she make after a month?
How did her coworker get involved?

title: The Broken Window
text: Two brothers named Jack and Sam were playing ball in the backyard. Jack threw the ball too hard and it went through the kitchen window. The boys stood still for a moment not knowing what to do. Their mother came outside when she heard the noise. Jack immediately told her what happened and said he was sorry. His mother appreciated that he told the truth and they called someone to fix the window that afternoon.
key_points: What happened and who was responsible?
How did the boys react at first?
What did Jack do when their mother came out?
How did the mother respond and what happened next?

title: The Delayed Flight
text: A family was traveling home after a vacation. Their flight was delayed by four hours due to bad weather. The children were tired and bored at the airport. Their father bought them each a book and some snacks. The children read quietly for most of the wait. When they finally boarded the plane the youngest child fell asleep before takeoff and did not wake up until they landed.
key_points: Why was the family stuck at the airport and how long was the delay?
How did the father keep the children occupied?
What happened when they finally boarded?

title: The New Puppy
text: A family adopted a puppy named Biscuit from the local shelter. The first night Biscuit cried and kept everyone awake. The father slept on the floor next to the puppy's bed so she would not feel alone. By the third night Biscuit slept through without making a sound. The children said Biscuit was the best thing that had ever happened to their family.
key_points: Where did the family get Biscuit and what was the first night like?
What did the father do to help?
How did things change by the third night?
What did the children say about Biscuit?

title: The Surprise Visit
text: A woman named Rose had not seen her sister in two years. One Saturday morning her doorbell rang and when she opened the door her sister was standing there with a suitcase. Rose burst into tears and hugged her. Her sister had taken a last minute flight as a surprise. They spent the whole weekend catching up and did not want it to end.
key_points: How long had it been since Rose saw her sister?
What happened on Saturday morning?
Why had the sister come and how did Rose react?
How did they spend the time together?

title: The Long Queue
text: A man named Frank arrived at the post office to mail an important package. The line was very long and moved slowly. After forty minutes he finally reached the counter. The clerk told him he had filled out the wrong form. Frank filled out the correct form and the clerk processed everything quickly. Frank made it back to work just in time for his afternoon meeting.
key_points: Why was Frank at the post office and what was the line like?
What problem did he encounter at the counter?
How did it get resolved and did he make it back in time?

title: The Camping Trip
text: A group of friends went camping for the first time. They struggled to put up the tent and it took them almost two hours. When they finally got it up it started to rain. They all crowded inside and told stories and jokes all evening. The next morning the sun came out and they spent the day hiking. Everyone agreed it was one of the best weekends they had ever had.
key_points: What challenge did the group face when setting up camp?
What happened once the tent was up?
What did they do inside and how was the next day?
How did everyone feel about the weekend overall?

title: The Forgotten Appointment
text: A woman named Patty had a dentist appointment on Wednesday morning. She forgot to write it down and scheduled a lunch with a friend for the same time. On Wednesday morning the dentist's office called to confirm. Patty realized her mistake and called her friend to reschedule. Her friend was understanding and they moved the lunch to Friday. Patty went to the dentist and left a reminder in her phone for future appointments.
key_points: What mistake did Patty make and how did she find out?
What did she do to fix the situation?
How did her friend react?
What did Patty do to avoid this in the future?

title: The Science Fair
text: A girl named Zoe built a volcano for her school science fair. She worked on it every day for three weeks. On the day of the fair her volcano erupted perfectly in front of the judges. She explained how volcanoes work in simple terms. The judges awarded her second place. Zoe was happy and her parents took her out for ice cream to celebrate.
key_points: What did Zoe build and how long did she work on it?
How did her presentation go?
What was the result and how did her family celebrate?

title: The Borrowed Book
text: A man named George lent his favorite book to a colleague at work. Months passed and the colleague never mentioned returning it. George finally asked about it one afternoon. His colleague looked embarrassed and admitted he had accidentally left it on a train. He bought George a brand new copy and apologized. George said the apology meant more to him than the book.
key_points: What had George done and what problem came up months later?
What did his colleague admit had happened?
How did the colleague make it right and how did George respond?

title: The Cooking Class
text: A retired man named Arthur decided to take a cooking class to learn how to make pasta from scratch. He had never cooked much before. In the first class he made a mess and his pasta fell apart. By the fourth class he had made a dish that impressed his instructor. He invited his daughter over for dinner and cooked the pasta for her. She asked him for the recipe.
key_points: Why did Arthur take a cooking class and what was he learning?
How did his first class go?
What improvement did he show by the fourth class?
What happened when he cooked for his daughter?

title: The Missing Scarf
text: A woman named Clara lost her favorite scarf and was certain she had left it at a café. She went back the next morning to ask. The café owner said no scarf had been handed in. Two days later Clara found it on the floor behind her bedroom door. She had walked past it several times without noticing. She called the café to let them know and the owner laughed.
key_points: Where did Clara think she had lost her scarf and what did she do?
What did the cafe owner say?
Where did she eventually find the scarf?
What did she do after finding it?

title: The Snowy Morning
text: One winter morning a man named Owen looked outside and saw that it had snowed overnight. His car was buried under a thick layer of snow. He spent thirty minutes clearing it before he could leave. His neighbor saw him struggling and came over to help with another shovel. Together they cleared both cars in half the time. Owen brought his neighbor a hot drink to say thank you.
key_points: What problem did Owen face when he woke up?
Who helped him and how?
How did Owen thank his neighbor?

title: The Wrong Suitcase
text: After a long flight a woman named Nora picked up her suitcase from the baggage carousel and went home. When she opened it she found it was full of someone else's clothes. She called the airline right away. They told her another passenger had picked up her suitcase by mistake. Both suitcases were exchanged the following morning. Nora and the other passenger laughed about it when they spoke on the phone.
key_points: What mistake did Nora make at the airport?
How did she discover the problem?
How was the situation resolved?
How did the two passengers react when they spoke?

title: The Parking Ticket
text: A woman named Beth parked her car downtown to run a quick errand. She thought she had put enough money in the meter. When she returned she found a parking ticket on her windshield. She checked the meter and saw she had been five minutes over the limit. She paid the fine online that evening. After that she always set a timer on her phone whenever she parked.
key_points: What happened when Beth returned to her car?
What had gone wrong?
What did she do about it?
What new habit did she start after that?

title: The Choir Audition
text: A shy boy named Eli decided to audition for the school choir even though he was nervous. His voice shook a little at the start but he kept going. The music teacher smiled and told him he had a lovely voice. Eli was accepted into the choir. At the first concert his parents sat in the front row. When he saw them smiling he felt completely calm and sang better than he ever had before.
key_points: How did Eli feel going into the audition and how did it start?
What did the music teacher say?
What happened at the first concert?
How did seeing his parents affect his performance?

title: The Broken Bike
text: A girl named Penny was riding her bike to her friend's house when the chain came off. She did not know how to fix it. A man walking by stopped and asked if she needed help. He fixed the chain in just a few minutes. Penny thanked him and rode the rest of the way without any trouble. When she got home she asked her dad to teach her how to fix a bike chain herself.
key_points: What happened to Penny on her way to her friend's house?
Who helped her and how quickly was it fixed?
What did she ask her dad when she got home?

title: The Locked Door
text: A man named Phil came home from work and realized he had locked his keys inside the apartment. He called his landlord but there was no answer. He sat outside on the steps for an hour. His neighbor returned home and noticed him sitting there. The neighbor had a spare key that the previous tenant had left. It worked and Phil was able to get inside. He had a spare key made the very next day.
key_points: What was Phil's problem when he got home?
What did he try first and what happened?
How did his neighbor help?
What did Phil do the very next day?

title: The Art Exhibition
text: A woman named Fiona had been painting as a hobby for three years. Her friend encouraged her to enter a local art exhibition. Fiona was nervous but submitted two of her paintings. On the evening of the exhibition she almost did not go. Her friend convinced her to come. One of her paintings was awarded a ribbon for most original work. Fiona cried when she found out.
key_points: What did Fiona's friend encourage her to do?
How did Fiona feel about going to the exhibition?
What was the outcome for one of her paintings?

title: The Hot Day
text: On the hottest day of the summer a boy named Tyler wanted ice cream. His mother drove him to three different stores but they were all sold out. On the way home they passed a neighbor who was selling homemade popsicles from her front yard for fifty cents each. Tyler bought two and said they were the best thing he had ever tasted. His mother bought one too.
key_points: What did Tyler want and what problem did they run into?
How many stores did they try?
How did they find a solution and how did Tyler feel about it?

title: The Train Seat
text: An elderly woman named Edna was standing on a crowded train with no seat available. A young man named Cole noticed her and immediately stood up to offer her his seat. Edna thanked him and sat down. She asked him where he was going and they ended up talking for the whole journey. When Edna got off she told him he had made her day. Cole felt good for the rest of the afternoon.
key_points: What did Cole notice and what did he do?
What happened between Cole and Edna on the journey?
What did Edna say when she got off and how did Cole feel?

title: The Recipe Mix-Up
text: A man named Hugo decided to bake cookies for his office. He followed the recipe carefully but accidentally used salt instead of sugar. No one said anything at first but the expressions on their faces told him something was wrong. His coworker finally admitted that the cookies tasted terrible. Hugo laughed and went to the bakery across the street and bought a box of proper cookies instead.
key_points: What was Hugo trying to do and what mistake did he make?
How did his coworkers react at first?
How did Hugo handle the situation in the end?

title: The Bird at the Window
text: A woman named Iris was working from home when a small bird flew into her window and fell onto the windowsill. She went outside carefully and picked it up. She put it in a small box with a soft cloth and kept it in a quiet room. Two hours later the bird was moving around. She took the box outside and opened it. The bird sat for a moment and then flew away.
key_points: What happened while Iris was working at home?
What did she do to help?
How did the story end?

title: The Late Night Study
text: A university student named Marcus stayed up until three in the morning studying for an exam. He was so tired by the end that he could barely read his notes. He set his alarm and went to sleep. The next morning he felt clearer and remembered everything he had studied. He passed the exam with a good grade. He told his roommate he would never leave studying to the last night again.
key_points: What did Marcus do to prepare for his exam and how did he feel afterward?
How did he feel the next morning?
What was the result and what did he tell his roommate?

title: The Neighborhood Clean-Up
text: A woman named Sandra organized a clean-up day for her neighborhood. She posted flyers and expected just a few people to show up. On the morning of the event thirty neighbors arrived with bags and gloves. They cleaned the whole park in two hours. The local newspaper sent someone to take a photo. Sandra was featured in the paper the following week.
key_points: What did Sandra organize and how many people did she expect?
What actually happened on the day?
What was the unexpected outcome for Sandra?

title: The Rain on Moving Day
text: A young couple named Dan and Amy chose a Saturday to move into their new apartment. On that morning it rained heavily. They carried boxes through the rain and got soaked. Their friends who had come to help were good-natured about it and joked the whole time. By the afternoon the rain stopped and they ordered pizza for everyone. Dan said it was a moving day story they would tell for the rest of their lives.
key_points: What did Dan and Amy plan and what went wrong with the weather?
How did their friends handle the situation?
What did they do when the rain stopped?
How did Dan describe the day afterward?
